description: Default architecture, no weight assignment (random init, expected ~0% accuracy).

2-layer transformer with LN bypassed. Layer-0 attention gathers x1,y1,x2,y2,xq one-hots into dedicated residual slots; layer-0 MLP is a (m,b,x1,x2)-indexed lookup writing m,b one-hots; layer-1 MLP is an (m,b,xq,pos)-indexed lookup writing yq tens digit at pos 11 and ones digit at pos 12.

Same circuit as v1 (LN bypassed, attention-gather + lookup-MLP factorization for m,b detection and yq emission), but with the residual stream shrunk to d_model=128 and d_ff=4500 to remove the slack from v1.

2-layer transformer (LN bypassed). Layer-0 MLP factorizes m,b detection as an OR over (x, m*x+b) hits per input pair, accumulating a 'passes-through' score per (m,b) line. The unique (m,b) consistent with BOTH points ends at score 1.0; lines through one point only sit at 0.5. Layer-1 MLP gates on score==1.0 to look up the correct yq digit per position. d_model=152, d_ff=1000.

Same circuit as v3 but the second block has no attention module (Block constructed with use_attn=False), removing ~92K dead attention parameters from the unused second-layer attention while keeping 100% accuracy.
